title: US threatens to destroy Iran if it tries to assassinate its president and contacts on Hormuz resume
summary: President Trump ordered an unprecedented bombing as retaliation, while the Iranian foreign minister visits Oman to resume talks on free navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Yui Kamiji achieves the Golden Grand Slam by winning Wimbledon wheelchair tennis
summary: Japanese tennis player Yui Kamiji won Wimbledon and completed the career Golden Slam, adding the only title she was missing in her category.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi promises to be a 'combative politician' like Abe at a tribute event
summary: At a rally in memory of Shinzo Abe, the leader stated that she will face criticism and fight for her convictions, following the legacy of the former prime minister.

title: Typhoon 9 causes injuries and power outages in Okinawa as it moves away from the islands
summary: Strong Typhoon No. 9 left 12 injured and 27,000 power outages in Okinawa, with warnings of high waves and possible landslides in the Sakishima Islands.

title: Extreme heat wave: 39.3 degrees in Dazaifu and record temperatures in western Japan
summary: The city of Dazaifu recorded 39.3°C, one of the highest temperatures of the year, and intense heat is expected to continue next week in large areas of western Japan.

title: JAXA successfully tests a reusable rocket that takes off and lands vertically
summary: The Japanese space agency's experimental vehicle RV-X rose 11 meters and landed successfully, confirming the viability of reusable technology to reduce costs.

title: Shohei Ohtani misses the All-Star Game due to inflammation in his left knee
summary: The Dodgers player regretted having to drop out of the All-Star due to an injury, but is approaching 350 combined home runs in Japan and MLB.

title: Japanese pianist Kayoko Muroi dies at 105, active until the end
summary: Muroi, who studied with Wilhelm Kempff and performed internationally, died at 105, leaving a legacy as one of the longest-active pianists.

title: Prince Harry meets privately with King Charles and his family for the first time in four years
summary: During a charity visit to the United Kingdom, Harry and Meghan Markle met with King Charles and Queen Camilla in a private meeting.

title: China and North Korea exchange congratulations on the 65th anniversary of their mutual aid treaty
summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Kim Jong-un reaffirmed their commitment to strengthen strategic relations on the occasion of the pact signed in 1961.

title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalist for reports on Air Force One
summary: The US administration demands that the journalist testify about his reports on the presidential plane, which drew criticism for press pressure.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 4,000 dead and Japan sends rescue team
summary: The large-magnitude earthquake caused devastation with thousands of victims, while the search for missing persons continues with international aid.

title: Ruling party pushes Osaka 'sub-capital' bill and asks opposition for concessions
summary: The LDP urges opposition parties to give way to pass the bill that aims to make Osaka a sub-capital, while the PDP criticizes the way of legislating.

title: Meta suspends feature that used Instagram photos to train artificial intelligence after backlash
summary: After strong criticism from users, Meta deactivated after three days the tool that allowed using Instagram images as AI material without consent.

title: Fatal accident on Toda highway: trailer's load detaches and kills driver
summary: On the Toda bypass road in Saitama, a truck spilled its load of steel beams and the driver died after a sudden brake.

title: Police inspector arrested for kicking a motorcycle in Sendai while on vacation
summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Police officer was arrested for damaging a parked motorcycle on public road, in an act caught on cameras.

title: Bears break into homes in Japan in search of food, causing alarm
summary: In Iwate Prefecture, several incidents of bears entering homes and stealing food have been recorded, leading authorities to issue warnings.

title: Kazuma Okamoto ties Shohei Ohtani's NPB rookie home run record with 22
summary: The Yomiuri Giants slugger hit his 22nd homer, tying the best mark for a rookie in Japanese professional baseball.

title: Spain beats Belgium and reaches the World Cup semi-finals for the first time in four editions
summary: With a goal from substitute Mikel Merino, Spain won a tight match to qualify among the top four of the tournament.

title: Summer season opens at Nagashima Spa Land's giant pool
summary: Nagashima Spa Land's saltwater pool, one of the largest in Japan, opened the summer season with bathers enjoying the water.
